title: Treat coordination read models and queue models as lagging caches instead of blocking writes
summary: Stop rebuilding and saving coordination read models and queue models synchronously on every mutation. Key them by authoritative revision, refresh them lazily or in background workers, and surface staleness explicitly when a reader outruns the cache.

acceptance.label: Hot-path mutations no longer save coordination read models or queue models synchronously
Readers can tell whether a served read model is current, stale-but-acceptable, or needs rebuild
Read-model rebuilds remain correct across restarts, compaction, and concurrent authoritative mutations
